summary: Building a Naturavini wine price list app, starting with a simplified version: basic wine list table with country, winery, wine name, and price, plus add/edit/delete functionality. More features to be added incrementally.
architectural_notes: Build incrementally: start with basic table and CRUD, then layer in language toggle, PDF export, and styling
known_issues: Previous build attempts were too complex and hit processing limits — simplified approach adopted
Add Wine modal background is still transparent — confirmed recurring issue after version 2
Version 3 modal fix made things worse — a black screen was added but the modal background is still not solid/opaque
Version 5: all added wines are lost (data not persisting) and Add Wine functionality is broken — wines cannot be added
PDF export is broken: same data/rendering issue as the modal, and the Naturavini logo is missing from the PDF output
Wine data inserted by the user was lost — needs to be restored/re-added

existing_features.title: Build a frontend wine list table that displays all wines grouped by country, with columns: Winery, Wine Name, and Price. The table must be readable and well-structured.

Implement Add, Edit, and Delete functionality for wines in the frontend. Use a modal or inline form for adding and editing a wine (fields: country, winery, wine name, price). Deleting should prompt a confirmation before removing.

Apply a cohesive visual theme inspired by the Naturavini brand: olive green section headers/banners, tangerine orange accents for buttons and highlights, clean serif or elegant sans-serif typography, and a light neutral background. The overall feel should be premium and wine-catalogue-like.
Display the Naturavini logo (generated image) prominently in the page header.
Add a 'Grape Variety' text input field to the Add/Edit Wine modal form. The field should be optional and placed between the wine name and price fields.

Fix the Add/Edit Wine modal so its background is fully opaque. The modal dialog panel must have a solid white (or light neutral) background so the page content behind it is not visible through the modal.
Add a 'Wine Style' dropdown/select field to the Add/Edit Wine modal form. Options must include: Red, White, Rosé, Sparkling, Orange. The field should be placed after the wine name field.
Add a 'Region' text input field to the Add/Edit Wine modal form. The field should be placed after the country field.

Add 'Pét-Nat' as an option to the Wine Style dropdown/select field in the Add/Edit Wine modal form (frontend/src/components/WineFormModal.tsx). The updated options must be: Red, White, Rosé, Sparkling, Orange, Pét-Nat.

Fix the Add/Edit Wine modal overlay so it is fully opaque. The modal backdrop/overlay must be a solid, non-transparent color so the page content behind the modal is not visible through it. Ensure both the overlay and the dialog panel have solid, opaque backgrounds.

In the wine list table (frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx), apply the same font styling to the Wine Name column as is used for the Winery column, so both display in the same typeface, weight, and size.

Fix the Add/Edit Wine modal in frontend/src/components/WineFormModal.tsx by removing any black overlay or backdrop that obscures the page, and replacing it with a clean, fully opaque solid white or light neutral background on the dialog panel itself. The DialogOverlay must not render a dark or black screen. The modal should appear as a standard dialog floating above the page with a solid, opaque panel background — no transparency, no black screen, no see-through effect.

Add a 'Export to PDF' button to the wine list page (frontend/src/pages/WineList.tsx or frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx) that generates and downloads a formatted PDF of the complete wine catalogue, grouped by country, with columns for Winery, Wine Name, Wine Style, Grape Variety, Region, and Price. The PDF must include the Naturavini logo at the top and reflect the brand's visual identity (olive green headings, clean typography). Use a client-side PDF library (e.g. jsPDF with autoTable) — no backend involvement required.

Update the Naturavini logo image used in the page header (frontend/src/components/PageHeader.tsx) to a regenerated version that closely matches the original Natura Vini pricelist logo — a refined, premium wine brand mark with the 'Natura Vini' wordmark and any accompanying graphical elements (e.g. leaf, vine, or botanical motif). The logo image must be stored in frontend/public/assets/generated/ and rendered in the header as before.

Fix the Add Wine functionality so that calling addWine from the frontend successfully creates and stores a new wine entry in the backend. Diagnose and resolve any mismatch between the frontend's actor call (in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts) and the backend's addWine function signature in backend/main.mo. The Add Wine form submission must result in the new wine appearing in the wine list.

Fix the PDF export in frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx so that wines are correctly grouped by country (each country appears only once, with all its wines listed under it). The current bug causes wines from the same country to appear in separate repeated sections (e.g. Italy appears twice, each with one wine instead of being merged into one group).

Fix the PDF export in frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx so that the Naturavini logo image (stored at frontend/public/assets/generated/naturavini-logo.png) is rendered at the top of the exported PDF, above the wine catalogue content, reflecting the brand's visual identity.

Add a 'Sold Out' checkbox/toggle to the Add/Edit Wine modal form (frontend/src/components/WineFormModal.tsx). The field should be placed near the bottom of the form before the submit button, labelled 'Sold Out'. It must be included in the form submission payload sent to the backend.

In the wine list table (frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx), display a visible 'Sold Out' badge or label on each wine row where soldOut is true. The indicator should be styled consistently with the Naturavini brand (e.g. a small tangerine-orange or muted red pill/tag with the text 'Sold Out'), placed in or near the Wine Name column.

Update the PDF export in frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx so that wines marked as soldOut display a 'SOLD OUT' text marker or stamp next to their entry in the exported PDF. The marker should be visually distinct (e.g. bold, coloured, or in parentheses) so it is clearly identifiable in the printed catalogue.

Add a 'Year' (vintage year) numeric input field to the Add/Edit Wine modal form (frontend/src/components/WineFormModal.tsx). The field should be optional, accept a 4-digit year (e.g. 2023), and be placed after the Wine Name field and before the Wine Style field. It must be included in the WineFormData interface and sent as part of the form submission payload.

Update the frontend wine query hooks in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts to include the 'year' field when calling addWine and updateWine, and to read it from getAllWines results. Also display the Year value in the wine list table (frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx) as part of the Wine Name column (e.g. appended in parentheses or as a small label), if present.

Update the page header (frontend/src/components/PageHeader.tsx) to display the newly generated uploaded-style logo image (a wine glass illustration with grape cluster and 'NATURA VINI' wordmark). The logo image must be stored as frontend/public/assets/generated/naturavini-logo-v2.png and rendered in the header. If image generation fails or the image cannot be loaded, fall back gracefully to the existing logo or plain text.

Update the PDF export in frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx to use the new logo image (frontend/public/assets/generated/naturavini-logo-v2.png) at the top of the exported PDF. If the logo image cannot be loaded at PDF generation time, skip rendering the logo in the PDF rather than throwing an error.

Update the PDF export in frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx to include the 'Year' column in the exported wine catalogue table, placed after the Wine Name column. If a wine has no year value, the cell should display a dash (—).

Update the page header (frontend/src/components/PageHeader.tsx) to render the newly uploaded Natura Vini logo image stored as frontend/public/assets/generated/naturavini-logo-v3.png. This logo features a green wine glass with a purple grape cluster and the 'NATURA VINI' wordmark in dark purple on a white background. If the image fails to load, fall back gracefully to plain text.

Update the PDF export in frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx to use the newly uploaded logo image (frontend/public/assets/generated/naturavini-logo-v3.png) at the top of the exported PDF, above the wine catalogue content. If the logo image cannot be loaded at PDF generation time, skip rendering the logo rather than throwing an error.

Ensure the 'Year' (vintage year) numeric input field is present and functional in the Add/Edit Wine modal form (frontend/src/components/WineFormModal.tsx). The field must be optional, accept a 4-digit year (e.g. 2023), be placed after the Wine Name field and before the Wine Style field, and its value must be included in the form submission payload sent to the backend.

Update the page header (frontend/src/components/PageHeader.tsx) to render the newly uploaded Natura Vini logo image stored as frontend/public/assets/generated/naturavini-logo-v4.png. This logo shows a wine glass with a grape cluster illustration and the 'NATURA VINI' wordmark in dark/black text on a light background. If the image fails to load, fall back gracefully to plain text.

Update the PDF export in frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx to use the newly uploaded logo image (frontend/public/assets/generated/naturavini-logo-v4.png) at the top of the exported PDF, above the wine catalogue content. If the logo image cannot be loaded at PDF generation time, skip rendering the logo rather than throwing an error.

Remove the Naturavini logo from the PDF export in frontend/src/components/WineTable.tsx. The exported PDF must not include any logo image at the top or anywhere else in the document. The rest of the PDF content (wine catalogue grouped by country, all columns) must remain intact.

new_feature_requests.summary: Basic wine list table with country, winery, wine name, and price fields
Add, edit, and delete wines functionality
PDF export for sending to clients
Wine type icons and fancy styling (olive green banners, tangerine orange accents, Naturavini logo)

Create a backend data model and CRUD API for wines. Each wine record must have: id, country, winery, wineName, price (text), and createdAt. Expose functions: addWine, updateWine, deleteWine, and getAllWines.
Pre-load the app with a set of sample wines from Croatia and Italy (at least 3 per country) so the list is not empty on first load.
Add grape variety field to the Add Wine form/modal
Fix Add Wine modal background — make it opaque/solid so the page background doesn't show through confusingly
Add wine style field (e.g. Red, White, Rosé, Sparkling, Orange) to the Add Wine form
Add region field to the Add Wine form
Add 'Pét-Nat' as an option to the Wine Style dropdown
Fix Add/Edit Wine modal overlay — modal background is still transparent/see-through, needs fully opaque solid background
Match wine name font to winery font on the wine list page
Generate and display the Naturavini logo image prominently in the page header (frontend/src/components/PageHeader.tsx), rendering it from frontend/public/assets/generated/.

Fix the backend so that wine data persists across canister upgrades. The stable storage for wines in backend/main.mo must use stable variables (or a stable data structure) so that all wines added by users are retained after redeployment. Verify that getAllWines returns previously added wines after an upgrade.

Update the Naturavini logo image in the page header (frontend/src/components/PageHeader.tsx) to use the uploaded Natura Vini logo: a wine glass illustration with grape cluster and the 'NATURA VINI' wordmark in dark purple, on a white background. Store the generated image as frontend/public/assets/generated/naturavini-logo.png and render it in the header.
Fix PDF export: same issue as the modal (likely a rendering/data bug), and the PDF is missing the Naturavini logo — logo must appear at the top of the exported PDF
Add a 'Sold Out' flag/indicator to wines — togglable in the Add/Edit wine form, displayed visually on the wine list table in the app, and shown in the PDF export.

Add a 'soldOut' boolean field to the Wine data model in backend/main.mo. Update addWine and updateWine function signatures to accept this field, and ensure getAllWines returns it. Default value must be false for existing and new wines.
Add a 'Year' (vintage year) input field to the Add/Edit Wine modal form, placed appropriately in the form fields.

Add a 'year' optional field (nullable text or Nat) to the Wine data model in backend/main.mo. Update the addWine and updateWine function signatures to accept this field, and ensure getAllWines returns it. Default value must be null/empty for existing wines.
Remove the Naturavini logo from the PDF export — the exported PDF should not include any logo

Restore the wine entries previously inserted by the user that were lost. Re-populate the backend with the wines that were added before the data loss, ensuring they appear in the wine list on load. If the exact wine entries are not recoverable from context, seed the backend with the known sample wines (Croatia and Italy, at least 3 per country) as a baseline and ensure stable storage is in place so data persists across canister upgrades.
